documents.title: Australian Student Visa — Subclass 500
documents.summary: The Student visa (subclass 500) allows Omani scholars to study full-time in Australia at a CRICOS-registered education provider. Since 23 March 2024, all applicants must satisfy the Genuine Student (GS) requirement. From 1 January 2025, a Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) is mandatory with every application. Visa fees are AUD $2,000 from 1 July 2025.

scope.failureImpact: Visa refusal or cancellation, removal from Australia, scholarship revocation

timeline.T-6 months: Typical earliest application window before course start
timeline.Within 7 days of arrival: Provide residential address to your education provider
Arrange or confirm OSHC cover is active

details: From 1 January 2025, a CoE is required when submitting every Student visa application — a Letter of Offer alone is no longer sufficient
Your CoE is issued by your CRICOS-registered education provider through the PRISMS system
Each CoE covers a specific course at a specific institution — changing provider requires a new CoE and may require a new visa
Your CoE number is entered directly into your online visa application
If you have multiple CoEs (e.g., English course then degree), list the primary CoE first

commonViolations.text: Applying for visa with Letter of Offer only (no CoE)
commonViolations.consequence: Application deemed invalid and rejected from 1 January 2025
Visa breach, cancellation, and removal proceedings

controls: Confirm your education provider is listed on the CRICOS register (cricos.teqsa.gov.au) before accepting an offer
Request your CoE from your provider as soon as your place is confirmed
Keep your CoE number accessible throughout your studies

details: Replaced the Genuine Temporary Entrant (GTE) requirement from 23 March 2024
You must demonstrate that your primary intention is to study — not to use the student visa as a pathway to remain in Australia

You will be assessed on: your current circumstances in Oman, ties to family, community, employment and economic circumstances; why you chose this course and provider; how the qualification benefits you in Oman
Strong ties to Oman (family, employment prospects, MoHE sponsorship return obligation) support a genuine student finding
MoHE scholarship and return service obligation are strong evidence of genuine student intent
mitigation: Prepare a clear written statement explaining your study goals and how the qualification will benefit your career in Oman
Include evidence of MoHE sponsorship and your return service obligation
Document your ties to Oman (family, property, employment prospects)

details: Must study full-time at your enrolled CRICOS institution
Must notify your education provider of your Australian residential address within 7 days of arriving
Must maintain satisfactory attendance and course progression as per ESOS Act requirements
Must maintain adequate health insurance (OSHC) for the entire duration of your stay — see AU-OSHC-2026
Cannot work more than 48 hours per fortnight while your course is in session — see AU-WORK-RIGHTS-2026
Cannot enrol in another course that would compromise your principal course without provider approval
From 1 July 2025: AUD $2,000 per student visa application
Fees are non-refundable even if the application is refused
MoHE sponsorship may cover visa fees — confirm with your scholarship terms before applying
Family members who accompany you (dependants) must lodge separate visa applications with their own fees

links.title: CRICOS Register
links.description: Verify that your education provider is CRICOS-registered

complianceNotes: MoHE sponsorship and return obligation are among the strongest evidence you can provide for the Genuine Student requirement — include documentation in every visa application
Visa fees are reviewed annually — check the current fee at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au before applying
Your visa is tied to your CoE; any change in provider or course requires immediate action — contact your provider and Home Affairs

documents.title: Australia Student Visa — Work Rights
documents.summary: Student visa holders (subclass 500) may work up to 48 hours per fortnight while their course is in session. Students enrolled in a master's by research or doctoral degree have no work hour limits. Work restrictions do not apply during official course breaks. Breaching work conditions can result in visa cancellation.

appliesTo: All Omani students on subclass 500 student visa in Australia
scope.failureImpact: Visa cancellation, removal from Australia, scholarship revocation

details: Maximum 48 hours per fortnight (two-week period) while your course of study is in session
48 hours per fortnight is equivalent to approximately 3 days per week
A fortnight runs from Monday to the second Sunday — track your hours across the full 14-day period
All jobs combined must not exceed 48 hours in any fortnight during a study period
The restriction applies during exam periods as well as regular teaching weeks

commonViolations.text: Working more than 48 hours per fortnight during term
commonViolations.consequence: Visa breach condition 8105, visa cancellation, removal

commonViolations.text: Working before commencement of course (where not permitted by visa)

mitigation: Keep a fortnightly work hours log and share it with your employer if needed
Notify all employers of your visa work limit; employers may not be aware
Track hours from Monday to Sunday (second Sunday) — do not reset at month end

rules.rule: Unlimited Work — PhD and Research Master's
details: Students enrolled in a master's degree by research OR a doctoral degree (PhD) have no work hour limits at any time
This applies throughout the enrolment in the relevant research degree, including during term
If you change from a research degree to a coursework degree, the 48-hour limit re-applies
When your course of study is NOT in session (semester breaks, official holidays): no work hour limit
You may work full-time during semester breaks
If you take a supplementary or bridging course during a break (that counts toward your main course under CRICOS), the 48-hour limit applies unless the CRICOS listing explicitly exempts it
MoHE scholarship terms may restrict or prohibit employment — check your scholarship contract
Any employment income must be reported to MoHE annually and to the Australian Taxation Office (ATO)
You must have an Australian Tax File Number (TFN) before starting employment — apply at ato.gov.au

links.title: Work restrictions — Home Affairs
links.description: Official work conditions for student visa holders

links.title: Apply for a Tax File Number — ATO
links.description: Required before starting any paid employment in Australia

documents.title: Overseas Student Health Cover (OSHC) — Mandatory
documents.summary: All international students on a Student visa in Australia must maintain Overseas Student Health Cover (OSHC) for the entire duration of their stay. OSHC is a private health insurance product regulated by the Australian government. It covers most hospital and medical costs but not all services. Failure to maintain OSHC is a breach of visa conditions.

appliesTo: All Omani students on subclass 500 student visa in Australia
scope.failureImpact: Visa cancellation, major out-of-pocket medical expenses

details: Must be held for the full duration of your student visa — from arrival to departure
Must be purchased from an Australian government-approved OSHC provider
Approved OSHC providers include: Allianz Care Australia, BUPA Australia, Medibank, nib OSHC, and others — check privatehealth.gov.au for the current approved list
Many Australian universities have a preferred OSHC provider and may include OSHC in their offer letter — you can purchase through the university or directly
Covers: hospital treatment, GP visits (Medicare-equivalent), some specialist services, and ambulance in most states
Does NOT fully cover: dental, optical, physiotherapy — budget separately or take additional cover
authorizationSources: Department of Home Affairs OSHC requirements

commonViolations.text: Allowing OSHC to lapse during study period
commonViolations.consequence: Visa condition breach, uninsured medical liability

commonViolations.text: Purchasing OSHC from a non-approved provider
commonViolations.consequence: Does not satisfy visa condition — treated as no cover

controls: Set up auto-renewal on your OSHC policy before the first payment lapses
Keep your OSHC membership card accessible at all times
Extend OSHC before any visa extension to ensure continuous coverage

details: Register with a GP (General Practitioner) as soon as possible after arriving — some GPs bulk-bill OSHC, meaning no out-of-pocket cost
For emergencies: call 000 (ambulance, police, fire) or go to the nearest hospital Emergency Department
For urgent but non-life-threatening issues: many cities have 24-hour medical clinics
Present your OSHC membership card at every medical appointment
Keep all receipts for medical expenses — OSHC claims may require documentation
Lifeline: 13 11 14 (free, 24/7) — crisis support and suicide prevention
Beyond Blue: 1300 22 4636 (free) — mental health support
Headspace (headspace.org.au) — mental health support for people aged 12-25
All Australian universities have free student counselling services — use them; there is no stigma

Lifeline.name: Lifeline
Lifeline.description: Free, 24/7 crisis support and suicide prevention

links.title: OSHC — Study Australia
links.description: Overview of OSHC requirements and approved providers

complianceNotes: MoHE may cover OSHC costs as part of your scholarship — confirm this in writing before purchasing to avoid paying twice
OSHC does not give access to Australia's Medicare system (which is for citizens and permanent residents) — OSHC is the separate international student equivalent

documents.title: ESOS Act Compliance and Course Obligations
documents.summary: Under the Education Services for Overseas Students (ESOS) Act 2000, CRICOS-registered providers have specific reporting obligations regarding student attendance and academic progress. Students must maintain satisfactory attendance and academic progression. Providers must report breaches to the Department of Home Affairs, which can trigger visa cancellation.

appliesTo: All Omani students enrolled at CRICOS-registered Australian providers
scope.failureImpact: Visa cancellation, removal from Australia, scholarship termination

details: Must maintain satisfactory attendance as defined by your CRICOS provider — typically 80% or above
Your provider monitors attendance and must report to Home Affairs if you fall below required levels
Home Affairs may cancel your visa if your provider reports unsatisfactory attendance
Notify your provider if you are unwell or have a personal emergency — document everything to support any exemption claim
Extended absences require formal leave of absence approval from your provider

commonViolations.text: Dropping below 80% attendance without provider approval
commonViolations.consequence: Provider reports to Home Affairs, visa cancellation notice issued
Provider reports CoE cancellation, visa can be cancelled

mitigation: Contact your provider's Student Services immediately if personal circumstances are affecting attendance
Submit medical certificates or other evidence promptly for any absences
Know your provider's attendance monitoring policy — it is published in your student handbook

details: Must make satisfactory academic progress toward your qualification — as defined by your provider
If you fail the same unit twice or are at risk of not completing within your CoE timeframe, your provider must intervene and may cancel your CoE
Changing courses within the same provider generally requires a new CoE and must not delay your overall completion
MoHE minimum GPA requirements (3.0/4.0 for postgraduate) apply independently — you must satisfy both provider and MoHE standards
controls: Meet with your academic advisor or course coordinator if you fail any unit
Apply for Special Consideration if illness or personal circumstances affect assessments — do this before results are released, not after

details: Transferring to another CRICOS provider within your first 6 months of your principal course is restricted
Transfer within 6 months requires your current provider's written release OR evidence that remaining is not in your best interests
After 6 months: you can transfer, but must obtain a new CoE from the new provider and may need a new visa if your current visa lists your old provider
MoHE must approve any provider transfer — obtain written approval before initiating transfer
failureModes: Transferring without provider release within 6 months — both CoEs may be cancelled
Transferring without MoHE approval — scholarship violation

details: Must notify your provider of your Australian residential address within 7 days of arriving
Must notify your provider of any address changes within 7 days of moving
Must notify your provider if you intend to defer, suspend, or withdraw from your course — do this before stopping attendance
Providers are required to attempt to contact you before reporting you to Home Affairs

links.title: ESOS Act and National Code — TEQSA
links.description: Framework protecting rights of international students in Australia

links.title: Student visa conditions — Home Affairs
links.description: Full list of conditions attached to the subclass 500 student visa

complianceNotes: Australia's ESOS framework is the equivalent of SEVIS in the US — your provider is legally required to monitor and report you
Your rights under the ESOS framework include: access to your provider's complaints process before any CoE cancellation, the right to appeal provider decisions
The Overseas Students Ombudsman (oso.gov.au) investigates complaints about private providers — use this if your provider is not responsive

documents.title: Temporary Graduate Visa — Subclass 485 (Post-Study Work)
documents.summary: The Temporary Graduate visa (subclass 485) allows recent Australian graduates to live and work in Australia after completing their studies. It provides unlimited work rights. Multiple streams are available depending on your qualification level. CRITICAL for MoHE scholars: this visa does not override your scholarship return obligation — MoHE approval is required before applying.

appliesTo: Omani graduates of Australian CRICOS institutions considering post-study work
scope.failureImpact: MoHE scholarship repayment if applied without approval; visa refusal if eligibility criteria not met

timeline.T-6 months: Begin planning; consult MoHE about post-graduation options
timeline.At graduation: Apply before student visa expires; must be applied for while in Australia

details: Must have completed an eligible Australian qualification from a CRICOS-registered provider
Must meet the Australian Study Requirement: at least 2 years of registered study in Australia while holding an eligible visa
Must apply within 6 months of your course completion date
Must be in Australia when you apply
Must be under 50 years of age (unless applying under certain regional streams)
Your MoHE scholarship contract requires you to return to Oman within 60 days of completing your program
Applying for the subclass 485 visa does NOT override this contractual obligation
If you apply without MoHE written approval, you risk full scholarship repayment plus penalties
MoHE may grant written approval for a post-study work period — contact the Scholarship Department at least 6 months before graduation
failureModes: Applying for 485 without MoHE approval — scholarship repayment demanded
Missing the 6-month application window after course completion
mitigation: Contact MoHE Scholarship Office (scholarships@mohe.gov.om) 6 months before graduation to discuss post-study options
Obtain MoHE approval in writing before lodging the 485 application

details: Unlimited work rights — no hour restrictions
Can work in any occupation or industry
Can be employed, self-employed, or run a business
Can bring family members (dependants) who also receive work rights

links.title: Temporary Graduate visa (485) — Home Affairs

links.title: Post-Study Work stream — Home Affairs
links.description: Specific requirements for the post-study work stream

documents.title: Cultural Adaptation and Islamic Accommodations — Australia
documents.summary: Australia's major cities are multicultural, with well-established Muslim communities and Islamic facilities at most universities. Understanding Australian academic culture and campus life will support your success. Most Australian universities have dedicated international student support services.

scope.failureImpact: Social isolation, academic difficulties, wellbeing challenges

details: Most Australian universities have multi-faith prayer rooms and dedicated Islamic prayer spaces — check your university's campus map or student portal
Muslim Student Associations (MSAs) are active at all major Australian universities — join for community, halal food tips, and support
Halal food is widely available in major Australian cities (Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Adelaide, Perth); most universities have nearby halal restaurants and some campus options
Ramadan accommodation (alternative exam times, iftar events) is available — contact your student services and lecturers at the start of term
The National Halal Certification Authority (nhca.com.au) maintains a list of certified halal products and restaurants
controls: Contact your university's Muslim Student Association before arriving — most have WhatsApp groups for incoming students
Notify exam coordinators of Ramadan observance at the beginning of semester

details: Tutors expect students to think critically and form their own arguments — summarizing without analysis is not sufficient at university level
Class participation and tutorial discussion contribute to learning and sometimes to grades — active engagement is expected
Academic integrity is strictly enforced via Turnitin and other tools; all sources must be cited, including paraphrased ideas
Punctuality is expected; notify your tutor in advance if you will miss a class or tutorial
Australian universities use a 'student-centred' approach — you are expected to seek help proactively, not wait to be asked
AESG (Academic English Support Groups) and learning advisors are available at all universities for academic writing help
Under the ESOS framework, you have formal rights including access to your provider's complaints process before any CoE cancellation
The Overseas Students Ombudsman (oso.gov.au) handles unresolved complaints against registered private education providers
For public universities (most main campuses), contact the state or territory ombudsman
Seek help from your Student Association or International Student Office before escalating complaints
